findings.location: s1 — 複合キーを用いた排他制御の実装 / Form実装例
findings.description: The `getDeletedUsers()` method implementation in `UsersBulkDeleteForm` is entirely omitted. The source shows the complete logic: creating a `List<CompositeKey>` from `Arrays.asList(userCompositeKeys)`, iterating over all users, building a `CompositeKey` per user via `new CompositeKey(user.getUserId(), user.getPk2(), user.getPk3())`, and using `deletedCompositeKeys.contains(compositeKey)` to collect only checked users. This is the key mechanism for filtering selected rows and is decision-necessary.
findings.source_evidence: Source 'Action で使用する Form の実装例' code block shows the full `getDeletedUsers()` implementation including `Arrays.asList(userCompositeKeys)`, `CompositeKey` construction, and `contains()` check.

findings.location: s1 — 複合キーを用いたUIの実装 / JSP実装例
findings.description: The full JSP example is truncated to only the `n:compositeKeyCheckbox` tag. The source example also shows the pattern of using multiple `n:hidden` fields alongside `n:compositeKeyCheckbox` to preserve all row properties (userId, pk2, pk3, name, profile, version) via `form.users[${count-1}].xxx` during form submission. This hidden-fields pattern is part of the implementation guidance.
findings.source_evidence: Source JSP block shows six `<n:hidden name="form.users[${count -1}].userId" />` etc. fields alongside `n:compositeKeyCheckbox`.

findings.description: Source contains 4 headings (top-level title, 複合キーを用いたUIの実装, 複合キーを用いた排他制御の実装, Javascriptの使用) but the knowledge file merges the first three into a single section s1. The two h2-level source sections (UIの実装 and 排他制御の実装) are distinct topics with substantial content (well over 2000 characters combined), and merging them reduces topical findability.
findings.source_evidence: Automated warning S9 confirmed: source headings=4, knowledge sections=2.

findings.description: `ExclusiveUserCondition` is a PascalCase class name used repeatedly in multiple code examples (prepareVersions, checkVersion, updateVersionWithCheck calls) but is absent from s1's hints array.
findings.source_evidence: Source code blocks show `new ExclusiveUserCondition(row.getString("USER_ID"), ...)` in all three Action implementation examples.
